name: fastapi
summary: Modern, fast web framework for building APIs with Python type hints

story.problem: Building REST APIs in Python traditionally meant Flask (no async, manual validation, no auto-docs) or Django REST Framework (heavy, complex configuration). Developers spent more time wiring boilerplate than writing business logic.
story.mental_model: FastAPI is a router + validator + doc generator in one. You declare route functions with Python type hints — FastAPI reads those hints to validate requests, serialize responses, and generate a live Swagger UI automatically. Think of it as: write a typed function, get an API endpoint.
story.when_to_use: Building REST or async APIs in Python, microservices, ML model serving, or any backend where you want automatic request validation and OpenAPI docs out of the box.
story.when_not_to_use: Full-stack apps with server-rendered HTML — use Django instead. Very simple scripts that just need one endpoint — Flask is lighter. If your team is unfamiliar with Python type hints, the learning curve increases.

alternatives.name: flask
alternatives.reason: Simpler, synchronous, larger ecosystem of extensions, no type hints required

alternatives.name: django
alternatives.reason: Full-stack framework with ORM, admin UI, auth — for bigger apps

alternatives.name: starlette
alternatives.reason: The async foundation FastAPI is built on — use directly for lower-level control

alternatives.name: litestar
alternatives.reason: Similar type-hint-driven approach, opinionated but powerful

nodes.label: FastAPI
nodes.summary: The application. Create one instance, mount routers, add middleware.

nodes.label: APIRouter
nodes.summary: Group related endpoints. Include in the main app with a prefix.

nodes.label: Request
nodes.summary: Raw incoming HTTP request. Access headers, body, query params directly.

nodes.label: Response
nodes.summary: Low-level HTTP response. Usually you return a dict/Pydantic model instead.

nodes.label: Depends()
nodes.summary: Dependency injection. Declare shared logic (auth, DB sessions) once, reuse everywhere.

nodes.label: Body() / Query() / Path()
nodes.summary: Declare where a parameter comes from and add validation constraints.
nodes.signature: Body(..., embed=False) | Query(..., min_length=1) | Path(..., ge=0)

nodes.label: HTTPException
nodes.summary: Raise to return a specific HTTP error status with a JSON detail message.

nodes.label: BackgroundTasks
nodes.summary: Run work after the response is sent — emails, logging, cache invalidation.

nodes.label: BaseModel (Pydantic)
nodes.summary: Define request/response schemas with type hints. FastAPI validates automatically.
nodes.signature: class Item(BaseModel): name: str; price: float

steps.label: Create app
steps.code:
from fastapi import FastAPI

app = FastAPI()

steps.label: Run
steps.explanation: --reload restarts on file save. Visit http://localhost:8000/docs for the auto-generated Swagger UI.

tasks.title: Accept and validate a request body

steps.label: Define schema
steps.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel

class Item(BaseModel):
    name: str
    price: float
    in_stock: bool = True

steps.label: POST endpoint
steps.explanation: FastAPI reads the type hint, validates the JSON body, and gives you a typed Item object. Invalid input returns 422 automatically.

steps.label: Test
steps.code: # FastAPI generates a /docs UI — test directly in browser

steps.label: Path param
steps.explanation: The {item_id} in the path is extracted and cast to int automatically.

steps.label: Query param
steps.explanation: Parameters not in the path are treated as query string params: /items?skip=5&limit=20

steps.label: Define dependency
Inject into routes
steps.explanation: FastAPI calls verify_token before the route handler. Reuse it across any number of routes without repeating the check.

steps.label: Define task
Inject BackgroundTasks
steps.explanation: The response returns immediately. send_welcome_email runs after the client receives the 200.
